ncs_standard_definition.description_short: Collects, analyzes, and interprets agricultural data to inform policy and decision-making.
ncs_standard_definition.description_detailed: Agricultural Statisticians work with government agencies, research institutions, and private companies to gather and analyze data on crop yields, soil quality, weather patterns, and other factors affecting agricultural productivity. They use statistical techniques and software to identify trends, patterns, and correlations, and provide insights to stakeholders to improve agricultural practices and policies.
ncs_standard_definition.official_min_education: M.Sc. in Agriculture or related field

first_stepper.description: Pursue a degree in agriculture or a related field, and gain experience through internships or volunteer work.

choice_maker.description: Bridge the gap between a generic degree and a career in agricultural statistics by acquiring relevant certifications and skills.

future_builder.description: Build on existing experience in agricultural statistics by acquiring advanced skills and certifications.
future_builder.prerequisite_experience: 2-3 years of experience as an Agricultural Data Analyst

problem_solver_uturn.description: Switch to a career in agricultural statistics from an unrelated field by acquiring relevant skills and building a portfolio.
